content: Let me start by reading the CLAUDE.md file to understand my role, then read the progress and prompt files, and finally read my assigned Lean file and its blueprint chapter.

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (analogy / refactor / challenger), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) — use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)
19	- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning
20	- archon-refactor-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-refactor-agent.py` — invoke the refactor subagent on a directive file
21	- archon-analogy-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-analogy-agent.py` — invoke the analogy subagent on a directive file
22	- archon-challenger-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-challenger-agent.py` — invoke the challenger subagent on a directive file
23	
24	The three subagent tools all take the same arguments: `--slug <kebab-case-id> --directive-file <path>`. Each shells out to `archon subagent <name> ...`, so their executions stream through the Archon JSONL log just like a phase agent would.
25	
26	## Key Files & Permissions
27	
28	All state files are in `.archon/`:
29	
30	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger) | Review Agent | User |
31	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
32	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
33	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
34	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
35	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
36	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
37	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<role>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |
38	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
39	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
40	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (refactor may rename file path only) | read | **write** |
41	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | refactor: write (all files; protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); analogy/challenger: read-only on project source | do not edit | write (via comments) |
42	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
43	| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | challenger: **write** | do not edit | read |
44	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | analogy: **write** | do not access | read |
45	
46	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow, run by hand by the mathematician.** The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. The plan agent invokes the refactor subagent by writing a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/` and passing its path to `archon-refactor-agent.py --directive-file`. This keeps each loop-driven refactor independently logged and avoids any chance of stale-directive reuse. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise and prune them out as you rewrite.
47	
48	## Protected Declarations
49	
50	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
51	
52	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
53	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
54	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
55	
56	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
57	
58	The blueprint uses two active markers.
59	
60	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
61	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
62	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
63	
64	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between the prover and review phases each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves — let the script do it.
65	
66	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations remain the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
67	
68	## User Interaction
69	
70	Users provide hints in two places:
71	
72	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
73	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
74	
75	## Agent Roles
76	
77	### Plan Agent
78	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
79	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
80	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
81	- Optionally invoke the **subagents** (`analogy`, `refactor`, `challenger`) by shelling out to the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for canonical ordering, the directive format for each subagent, and the exact invocation pattern.
82	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<role>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
83	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
84	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
85	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
86	
87	### Prover Agent
88	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
89	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
90	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
91	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
92	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
93	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
94	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
95	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
96	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
97	
98	### Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger)
99	
100	These are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. The plan agent invokes them on demand by running the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, with `--slug <slug> --directive-file <path>`. Each subagent reads only the files the directive points it at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other plan-agent state — and writes a self-contained report.
101	
102	- **`refactor`** — read `.archon/prompts/refactor.md`. Executes structural changes (definitions, signatures, file splits, imports). Inserts `sorry` at broken proof sites; never fills proofs. Writes report to `.archon/task_results/refactor-<slug>.md`.
103	- **`analogy`** — read `.archon/prompts/analogy.md`. Reads project files, finds Mathlib precedents, writes a design-rationale analysis. Persistent output at `analogies/<slug>.md`; report at `.archon/task_results/analogy-<slug>.md`. Read-only on project source.
104	- **`challenger`** — read `.archon/prompts/challenger.md`. Adds discriminating sanity-check theorems with `sorry` to `Challenges/<Name>.lean`. Provers fill them later. Report at `.archon/task_results/challenger-<slug>.md`. Read-only on target files.
105	
106	The plan agent always reads the full report file after a subagent returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings. None of the subagents spawn other subagents.
107	
108	### Review Agent
109	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
110	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
111	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
112	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
113	- Maintain the **semantic** blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE: ...` annotations, stale `\notready` removal — in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch `\leanok`; it's handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you.
114	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
115	
116	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
117	
118	Two non-agent steps run automatically each iteration to reduce the burden on the agents above:
119	
120	- **Pre-compactors** (`compact-strategy`, `compact-task-pending`, `compact-task-done`, `compact-project-status`): run before plan / review, rewriting oversized state files in place while preserving every actionable detail. Configured under `compaction.*` in `.archon/config.json`. Skipped when files are below threshold.
121	- **`sync_leanok`**: runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status. Replaces what used to be a multi-page review-agent task.
122	
123	Both write inner-git commits (`archon[NNN/precompact/...]`, `archon[NNN/marker-sync]`) so their output is auditable and revertable.

3	## Current Stage
4	
5	prover  (iteration 065 — **close Čech substep(s) + begin Phase B closure**. Iter-064 delivered Acceptable mode on `BasicOpenCech.lean` (substep decomposition into 3 new sorries + ~77 LOC scaffolding) and Good mode on `Differentials.lean` (new file ~165 LOC, 8 sorries, compiles). Iter-065 dispatches two parallel prover objectives: continue closing sorries in `BasicOpenCech.lean` and begin closing sorries in `Differentials.lean`. Plan agent does NOT pre-verify candidate proof bodies; transient sorries explicitly acceptable.)

13	## Big-picture context
14	
15	This project formalizes the Jacobian of a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over a field, following Christian Merten's challenge (`references/challenge.lean`). The nine declarations in `archon-protected.yaml` are the deliverables — their signatures are frozen.
16	
17	`STRATEGY.md` describes the multi-phase build-out path (Phases A–E). After iter-064, Phase A's substantive Čech-acyclicity theorem has 4 remaining sorries in `Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean` (component (i), substep (a), substep (a) for `s₀`, substep (b2)+(c) for `s₀`). Phase B's `Differentials.lean` scaffold is created with 8 sorries. Phases C / D / E remain in their pre-iter-064 state.
18	
19	## Iteration 065 plan-agent summary (this iteration)

21	**Iter-064 verification.** Plan-agent verification this pass:
22

Pre-iter-064: `11` (or `13` counting only the new transient sorries in BasicOpenCech). Post-iter-064: `21`. The +10 change comprises: BasicOpenCech went `2 → 4` (+2 from decomposition), Differentials added 8 new sorries.

Iter-065 sends the prover back to this theorem with directive to **close at least one sorry** (preferred targets: component (i) at L662 or substep (a) at L444). Iter-065 dispatches the prover to attack **one or both** of these. A restriction-of-scalars helper (`X.Modules → Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)`) may be introduced if it unlocks downstream statements.

37	## Current Objectives
38	
39	### 1. **`AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean`** — close one or more sorries inside `basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf`
40	
41	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_MayerVietoris.tex` § *Čech acyclicity for the structure sheaf on affine basic-open covers*.
42	
43	Iter-064 prover report (**recommended pre-read**): `logs/iter-064/prover-BasicOpenCech-result.md` — contains the decomposition details, the 3-component trajectory, and next-iteration recommendations.
44	
45	Review-agent recommendations (session 64): `proof-journal/sessions/session_64/recommendations.md`.
46	
47	**Action**: Engage with the body of `basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf`. The proof now contains four labelled sorries:
48	
49	- **L444** — `substep (a)`: extra-degeneracy on the `s`-indexed slice cover. The claim is `∀ (f : s), (cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C) (slice cover at f)).ExactAt n`. Leverage chain: `FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech` → `SimplicialObject.Augmented.ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv` → op-passage via `CochainComplex.opEquivalence` → `ExactAt n` for `0 < n`.
50	- **L662** — `component (i)`: Čech-cohomology refinement transport `s → s₀`. The claim is a bidirectional `Function.Exact` equivalence between `K := cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C) (basicOpenCover s)` and `K₀ := cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C) (basicOpenCover ↑s₀)`. The inclusion `↑s₀ ↪ s` induces a cochain-complex map `K → K₀` by precomposing multi-indices. The two covers generate the same sieve (`⨆ basicOpenCover s = U = ⨆ basicOpenCover ↑s₀`), so exactness should transport.
51	- **L690** — `substep (a) for s₀`: extra-degeneracy on the `↑s₀`-indexed slice cover. Near-verbatim copy of L444 with `↑s₀` in place of `s`.
52	- **L720** — `substep (b2)+(c) for s₀`: finite product-localisation commutation + `IsLocalizedModule.Away` instances + `exact_of_isLocalized_span` on `↑s₀`. Because `s₀` is finite, `Fin (i+1) → ↑s₀` is finite, so product-localisation commutation holds.
53	
54	The prover should **prioritize whichever sorry they judge most tractable**. The review agent ranks component (i) at L662 as highest priority (most self-contained). The original substep (a) at L444 is the longest-standing open piece. Both are excellent targets.
55	
56	#### Notes for the prover
57	
58	- **File**: `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean` only. Do not modify any other `.lean` file.
59	- **Mathematical content**: in the blueprint chapter `Cohomology_MayerVietoris.tex`, in `analogies/cech-koszul-precedent.md`, and in the iter-060/061/062/063/064 prover task reports.
60	- **Existing scaffolding in scope**: all iter-016 → iter-063 declarations now live across the three split files; everything imported via `import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.MayerVietorisCover` is available. The `hs_fin` `have` block is at L609–611.
61	- **Transient sorries are explicitly acceptable.** If a sorry exceeds the session, leave a well-commented `sorry` naming it and any sub-components.
62	- **Internal helper lemmas / `noncomputable def`s are explicitly encouraged.**
63	
64	#### What success looks like
65	
66	**(Best)** One or more sorries fully closed. Sorry trajectory `21 → 20` or better. Component (i) closed would be the highest-impact single closure.
67	
68	**(Good)** Substantial new scaffolding landed on one or more sorries (e.g., the cochain-complex map `K → K₀` constructed as a `have`/`noncomputable def` for component (i); or the `ExtraDegeneracy` instance drafted for substep (a)). Sorry trajectory unchanged or +1 from finer decomposition.
69	
70	**(Acceptable)** A sorry decomposed into finer substeps with clear commentary and trajectory. Iter-066 pickup is unambiguous.
71	
72	**(Failure)** No engagement beyond iter-064: all four sorries unchanged, no new scaffolding, no structural progress.
73	
74	### 2. **`AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean`** — close `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf` and/or `universalDerivation`
75	
76	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex` (updated iter-065 with `\lean{...}` macros).
77	
78	Iter-064 prover report (**recommended pre-read**): `logs/iter-064/prover-Differentials-result.md` — contains Mathlib API points consulted, modelling decisions, and next-iteration recommendations.
79	
80	Review-agent recommendations (session 64): `proof-journal/sessions/session_64/recommendations.md`.
81	
82	**Action**: Attack the two highest-priority sorries in `Differentials.lean`:
83	
84	1. **`relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf` (L67)** — The presheaf of relative Kähler differentials is a sheaf in the Zariski topology. This follows from the fact that Kähler differentials commute with localization: for a ring map `A → B` and `f ∈ B`, we have `Ω_{B[1/f]/A} ≅ Ω_{B/A} ⊗_B B[1/f]`. The gluing axiom on affine opens is built from this localization compatibility. Search Mathlib for `KaehlerDifferential` localization lemmas.
85	
86	2. **`universalDerivation` (L86)** — The morphism of abelian presheaves `d : 𝒪_X → Ω_{X/S}`. On each affine open, this is the Mathlib universal derivation `KaehlerDifferential.D`. Converting the presheaf-level `Derivation'` (from `PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.derivation'`) into a morphism of abelian presheaves should be a matter of unpacking the `Derivation` structure.
87	
88	3. **(Optional)** A restriction-of-scalars helper converting `C.left.Modules` to `Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)` — this would unlock `serre_duality_genus` and any future `k`-module cohomology of `O_C`-modules.
89	
90	The prover may also opportunistically close other sorries (`cotangent_exact_sequence`, `smooth_iff_locally_free_omega`, `cotangent_at_section`) if the session permits, but **items 1 and 2 are the priority**.
91

97	  - `KaehlerDifferential A B : Type _` (`Mathlib.RingTheory.Kaehler.Basic`) — ring-level Kähler differential module.
98	  - `KaehlerDifferential.D : Derivation A B (KaehlerDifferential A B)` — universal derivation.
99	  - `PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.derivation'` — presheaf-level derivation.
100	  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules` and `Scheme.PresheafOfModules` — target categories.
101	- **Constraints**: do not introduce new `axiom` declarations. The file may compile with `sorry` bodies.

125	## Known dead ends (do not retry)
126	
127	- The 8 currently-blocked protected sorries (`Jacobian.lean` × 5, `AbelJacobi.lean` × 3) — gated on Phase C representability + Phase E geometric content + multi-iteration upstream Mathlib advances.
128	- `PicardFunctor.representable` (`Picard/Functor.lean` L185) — closing on the global-sections-approximate `LineBundle` is a forbidden shortcut.
129	- `LineBundle` direct refinement — gated on `MonoidalCategory X.Modules` (still absent in Mathlib).
130	- Vacuous-functor / vacuous-sheaf closures — destroy the downstream Picard/cohomology chain; rejected on sight.
131	- New `axiom` declarations — forbidden by plan-agent rules.
132	- **Closing `basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf` by assuming `s = ∅`, by replacing the structure sheaf with a constant sheaf, or by any other shortcut** — the prover must engage with the actual mathematical content. Transient sorries are preferred to vacuous closures.
133	- **Closing the theorem via the `n = 1` special case + induction through `HasCechToHModuleIso`** — iter-060 prover already considered this and rejected. Iter-065 stays on the `IsCechAcyclicCover` branch.
134	- **Pursuing the full Stacks 03OU Čech-to-cohomology spectral sequence** as the proof route — out of scope; ~600+ LOC.
135	- **Attempting the Čech-vs-derived comparison `HasCechToHModuleIso`** in the same iteration as the `IsCechAcyclicCover` substep work — separate parallel branch (later iteration).

16	/-! # Relative Kähler differentials for schemes
17	
18	This file constructs the sheaf of relative Kähler differentials `Ω_{X/S}`
19	of a morphism of schemes `f : X → S`, together with its universal derivation
20	`d : 𝒪_X → Ω_{X/S}`. It also states the cotangent exact sequence and the
21	characterisation of smoothness in terms of local freeness of `Ω`.
22	
23	The construction builds on Mathlib's ring-theoretic `KaehlerDifferential`
24	and the presheaf-of-modules differential construction
25	`PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'`.
26	
27	## Status (iteration 064 — scaffold)
28	
29	All main declarations have `sorry` bodies. Closure trajectory is estimated
30	at ~10 iterations per `STRATEGY.md`.
31	
32	## References
33	
34	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex`.
35	-/
36	
37	set_option autoImplicit false
38	
39	universe u
40	
41	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
42	
43	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
44	
45	variable {X Y S : Scheme.{u}}
46	
47	/-! ## The relative cotangent presheaf and sheaf -/
48	
49	/-- The relative cotangent **presheaf** of a morphism of schemes `f : X ⟶ S`.
50	
51	On each open `U ⊆ X`, the sections are the Kähler differential module of
52	the ring map `O_S(f(U)) → O_X(U)` induced by `f`. More precisely, we use
53	the inverse-image presheaf `f⁻¹ O_S` on `X` and the canonical map to `O_X`;
54	the Kähler differential construction then gives a presheaf of `O_X`-modules.
55	
56	Mathlib leverage: `TopCat.Presheaf.pullback` for `f⁻¹`,
57	`PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'`
58	for the presheaf of Kähler differentials. -/
59	noncomputable def relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (f : X ⟶ S) : X.PresheafOfModules :=
60	  let φ' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm
61	    f.c
62	  PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials' φ'
63	
64	/-- The presheaf of relative differentials is a **sheaf** in the Zariski
65	topology: Kähler differentials commute with localisation, which gives the
66	gluing axiom on affine opens. -/
67	theorem relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf (f : X ⟶ S) :
68	    Presheaf.IsSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat)
69	      (relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).presheaf := by
70	  sorry
71	
72	/-- The sheaf of relative differentials `Ω_{X/S}`.
73	
74	Packages the presheaf `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf` with the sheaf axiom
75	`relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf` into the standard `X.Modules` shape. -/
76	noncomputable def relativeDifferentials (f : X ⟶ S) : X.Modules :=
77	  ⟨relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f, relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf f⟩
78	
79	/-! ## Universal derivation -/
80	
81	/-- The universal derivation `d : 𝒪_X → Ω_{X/S}`.
82	
83	On each affine open `V = Spec B` over `Spec A`, this is the Mathlib universal
84	derivation `d_{B/A} : B → Ω_{B/A}`. It is an `A`-linear sheaf map satisfying
85	the Leibniz rule `d(ab) = a d(b) + b d(a)`. -/
86	def universalDerivation (f : X ⟶ S) :
87	    X.ringCatSheaf.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat ⟶
88	      (relativeDifferentials f).val.presheaf := by
89	  sorry
90	
91	/-! ## Cotangent exact sequence -/
92	
93	/-- Cotangent exact sequence for a composition of schemes `X ⟶ Y ⟶ S`.
94	
95	For `f : X ⟶ Y` and `g : Y ⟶ S`, there is an exact sequence of
96	quasi-coherent `𝒪_X`-modules
97	```
98	  f^* Ω_{Y/S} ⟶ Ω_{X/S} ⟶ Ω_{X/Y} ⟶ 0.
99	```
100	Built on affine charts from the Mathlib ring-level cotangent exact
101	sequence and glued via the compatibility above. -/
102	theorem cotangent_exact_sequence (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ S) :
103	    ∃ (α : (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj (relativeDifferentials g) ⟶
104	        relativeDifferentials (f ≫ g))
105	      (β : relativeDifferentials (f ≫ g) ⟶ relativeDifferentials f),
106	      CategoryTheory.ShortComplex.Exact
107	        (CategoryTheory.ShortComplex.mk α β (by sorry)) ∧
108	      CategoryTheory.Epi β := by
109	  sorry
110	
111	/-! ## Smoothness and local freeness of `Ω` -/
112	
113	/-- Smoothness of a finite-presentation morphism is equivalent to `Ω_{X/S}`
114	being locally free of the given rank.
115	
116	The forward direction is the Jacobian criterion; the converse follows from
117	the cotangent exact sequence and Nakayama's lemma applied at each point. -/
118	theorem smooth_iff_locally_free_omega (f : X ⟶ S)
119	    (hfp : AlgebraicGeometry.LocallyOfFinitePresentation f) (n : ℕ) :
120	    Smooth f ↔
121	      ∀ (x : X), ∃ (U : X.Opens), x ∈ U.1 ∧ IsAffineOpen U ∧
122	        let R := X.ringCatSheaf.presheaf.obj (.op U)
123	        let M := (relativeDifferentials f).val.obj (.op U)
124	        Module.Free (↑R) (↑M) ∧ Module.rank (↑R) (↑M) = n := by
125	  sorry
126	
127	/-! ## Cotangent space at a section -/
128	
129	/-- If `f : X ⟶ S` is smooth and `s : S ⟶ X` is a section of `f`, then the
130	cotangent space `s^* Ω_{X/S}` is a locally free `𝒪_S`-module of rank `n`.
131	In particular, if `S = Spec k` and `X` is smooth of relative dimension `n`,
132	the cotangent space at the corresponding `k`-point is an `n`-dimensional
133	`k`-vector space. -/
134	theorem cotangent_at_section (f : X ⟶ S)
135	    (hfp : AlgebraicGeometry.LocallyOfFinitePresentation f)
136	    (s : S ⟶ X) (hs : s ≫ f = 𝟙 S) (n : ℕ)
137	    (hsmooth : Smooth f) :
138	    ∀ (x : S), ∃ (U : S.Opens), x ∈ U.1 ∧ IsAffineOpen U ∧
139	      let R := S.ringCatSheaf.presheaf.obj (.op U)
140	      let M := ((Scheme.Modules.pullback s).obj (relativeDifferentials f)).val.obj (.op U)
141	      Module.Free (↑R) (↑M) ∧ Module.rank (↑R) (↑M) = n := by
142	  sorry
143	
144	/-! ## Serre duality genus equality -/
145	
146	/-- For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C` over a field `k`,
147	the dimension of `H^0(C, Ω_{C/k})` equals the dimension of `H^1(C, 𝒪_C)`,
148	and both equal the genus `g(C)`.
149	
150	This is the dimension-one case of Serre duality applied to the canonical
151	sheaf `ω_C = Ω_{C/k}`.
152	
153	Note: the right-hand side needs a conversion from `C.left.Modules` to
154	`Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)`; this is deferred to a future helper definition. -/
155	theorem serre_duality_genus {k : Type u} [Field k]
156	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) [IsIntegral C.left] [IsProper C.hom]
157	    (hsmooth : Smooth C.hom) :
158	    Module.rank k (HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 0) =
159	      Module.rank k
160	        (HModule k
161	          (sorry : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat k)) 0) := by
162	  sorry
163	
164	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

1	\chapter{The sheaf of relative differentials}
2	\label{chap:differentials}
3	
4	This chapter develops the sheaf of relative K\"ahler differentials
5	$\Omega_{X/S}$ of a morphism of schemes $X \to S$, starting from the
6	ring-theoretic K\"ahler differential module $\Omega_{B/A}$ already
7	available in Mathlib. The output is a quasi-coherent sheaf of
8	$\struct{X}$-modules characterised by its restriction to affine opens:
9	for any affine open $V = \Spec B \subseteq X$ lying over an affine open
10	$U = \Spec A \subseteq S$, the sections satisfy
11	\[
12	  \Omega_{X/S}(V) \;=\; \Omega_{B/A}.
13	\]
14	
15	The downstream role of this chapter is twofold. First, the smoothness
16	hypothesis on the curve $C \to \Spec k$ that appears in the Jacobian
17	definition and the Abel--Jacobi map amounts to the condition that
18	$\Omega_{C/k}$ is locally free of rank one; this characterisation is
19	what unlocks the dimension computation
20	$\genus(C) = \dim_k H^0(C, \Omega_{C/k}) = \dim_k H^1(C, \struct{C})$.
21	Second, the cotangent space at the identity section of $\Jac(C)$ is
22	$\Omega_{\Jac(C)/k}$ pulled back along the unit $e \colon \Spec k \to
23	\Jac(C)$; the relative dimension of $\Jac(C)$ that appears in the
24	smoothness statement is read off from this tangent space.
25	
26	\section{The relative cotangent sheaf}
27	
28	\begin{definition}[Relative cotangent presheaf of an algebra]
29	  \label{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf}
30	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf}
31	  Let $f \colon X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes. For each pair of
32	  affine opens $U \subseteq S$, $V \subseteq X$ with $f(V) \subseteq U$,
33	  write $A = \struct{S}(U)$ and $B = \struct{X}(V)$, and use the
34	  $f$-induced ring map $A \to B$. The K\"ahler differential module
35	  $\Omega_{B/A}$ is then a $B$-module, hence a section over $V$ of a
36	  presheaf on $X$. The collection of these modules, together with the
37	  natural restriction maps along inclusions of affine opens, defines a
38	  presheaf of $\struct{X}$-modules on $X$.
39	\end{definition}
40	
41	\begin{definition}[Relative cotangent sheaf $\Omega_{X/S}$]
42	  \label{def:relative_kaehler_sheaf}
43	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.relativeDifferentials}
44	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf}
45	  The presheaf above is in fact a sheaf in the Zariski topology on
46	  $X$: the K\"ahler differential module commutes with localisation,
47	  which gives the gluing axiom on affine opens, and the descent to
48	  non-affine opens is standard. The resulting sheaf of
49	  $\struct{X}$-modules is the \emph{sheaf of relative differentials}
50	  $\Omega_{X/S}$. It is quasi-coherent.
51	\end{definition}
52	
53	\begin{remark}[Comparison with Mathlib's ring-level $\Omega_{B/A}$]
54	  \label{rem:kahler_compatibility}
55	  Mathlib provides the ring-theoretic K\"ahler differential module
56	  $\Omega_{B/A}$ together with the universal derivation $\mathrm{d}
57	  \colon B \to \Omega_{B/A}$. The construction of $\Omega_{X/S}$ on
58	  affine opens is, by definition, this Mathlib module. The
59	  derivative-of-localisation isomorphism $\Omega_{B[1/f]/A} \cong
60	  \Omega_{B/A} \otimes_B B[1/f]$ is precisely what makes the
61	  affine-restriction presheaf into a sheaf.
62	\end{remark}
63	
64	\section{Universal property and the cotangent exact sequence}
65	
66	\begin{definition}[Universal derivation]
67	  \label{def:universal_derivation}
68	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.universalDerivation}
69	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_sheaf}
70	  The morphism of sheaves of abelian groups
71	  $\mathrm{d} \colon \struct{X} \to \Omega_{X/S}$, defined on each
72	  affine open $V = \Spec B$ over $\Spec A$ by the Mathlib universal
73	  derivation $\mathrm{d}_{B/A} \colon B \to \Omega_{B/A}$, is the
74	  \emph{universal derivation}. It is an $A$-linear sheaf map satisfying
75	  the Leibniz rule $\mathrm{d}(ab) = a \mathrm{d}(b) + b \mathrm{d}(a)$.
76	\end{definition}
77	
78	\begin{theorem}[Cotangent exact sequence for a composition]
79	  \label{thm:cotangent_exact_sequence}
80	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.cotangent_exact_sequence}
81	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_sheaf, def:universal_derivation}
82	  For schemes $X \xrightarrow{f} Y \xrightarrow{g} S$, there is an
83	  exact sequence of quasi-coherent $\struct{X}$-modules
84	  \[
85	    f^*\Omega_{Y/S} \;\longrightarrow\; \Omega_{X/S} \;\longrightarrow\;
86	      \Omega_{X/Y} \;\longrightarrow\; 0,
87	  \]
88	  built on affine charts from the Mathlib ring-level cotangent exact
89	  sequence and glued via the compatibility above.
90	\end{theorem}
91	
92	\section{Smoothness and local freeness of $\Omega$}
93	
94	\begin{theorem}[Smoothness in terms of $\Omega$]
95	  \label{thm:smooth_iff_locally_free_omega}
96	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.smooth_iff_locally_free_omega}
97	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_sheaf}
98	  Let $f \colon X \to S$ be a finite-presentation morphism of schemes.
99	  Then $f$ is smooth of relative dimension $n$ if and only if
100	  $\Omega_{X/S}$ is locally free of rank $n$ as a sheaf of
101	  $\struct{X}$-modules. The forward direction is the Jacobian
102	  criterion; the converse follows from the cotangent exact sequence
103	  and Nakayama's lemma applied at each point.
104	\end{theorem}
105	
106	\begin{corollary}[Cotangent space at a section]
107	  \label{cor:cotangent_at_section}
108	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.cotangent_at_section}
109	  \uses{thm:smooth_iff_locally_free_omega}
110	  If $f \colon X \to S$ is smooth of relative dimension $n$ and
111	  $s \colon S \to X$ is a section of $f$, then the cotangent space
112	  $s^*\Omega_{X/S}$ is a locally free $\struct{S}$-module of rank $n$.
113	  In particular, if $S = \Spec k$ is a field point and $X$ is smooth of
114	  relative dimension $n$, the cotangent space at the corresponding
115	  $k$-point is an $n$-dimensional $k$-vector space.
116	\end{corollary}
117	
118	\section{Curves and the canonical sheaf}
119	
120	The case of a smooth proper curve $C \to \Spec k$ is the special case
121	$n = 1$: the relative cotangent sheaf $\Omega_{C/k}$ is a line bundle
122	on $C$, classically denoted $\omega_C$ and called the \emph{canonical
123	sheaf}.
124	
125	\begin{theorem}[Serre-duality genus equality]
126	  \label{thm:serre_duality_genus}
127	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.serre_duality_genus}
128	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_sheaf}
129	  Let $C$ be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over a
130	  field $k$. Then
131	  \[
132	    \dim_k H^0(C, \Omega_{C/k}) \;=\; \dim_k H^1(C, \struct{C}).
133	  \]
134	  Both sides equal the genus $\genus(C)$. The equality is the dimension
135	  one case of Serre duality applied to the canonical sheaf; combined
136	  with the definition $\genus(C) = \dim_k H^1(C, \struct{C})$, this
137	  provides the alternative differential-theoretic characterisation used
138	  when computing the relative dimension of $\Jac(C)$ at the identity.
139	\end{theorem}
140

the Kähler differential construction then gives a presheaf of `O_X`-modules.

Let me now think about the proof strategy. The `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf` is built from `PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials' φ'`. Looking at the `relativeDifferentials'_obj` lemma, for any open `U`, the sections are `CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential (φ'.app (op U))`.

The sheaf condition says that given a cover `U = ⋃ᵢ Uᵢ`, sections over `U` correspond to compatible families of sections over each `Uᵢ`. For Kähler differentials, this follows from the fact that Kähler differentials commute with localization.

In Mathlib, there might already be a result that `relativeDifferentials'` is a sheaf, or there might be a way to prove it using the localization properties. Let me search more specifically.

6	module

8	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf
9	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Differentials.Basic
10	
11	/-!
12	# The presheaf of differentials of a presheaf of modules
13	
14	In this file, we define the type `M.Derivation φ` of derivations
15	with values in a presheaf of `R`-modules `M` relative to
16	a morphism of `φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R` of presheaves of commutative rings
17	over categories `C` and `D` that are related by a functor `F : C ⥤ D`.
18	We formalize the notion of universal derivation.
19	
20	Geometrically, if `f : X ⟶ S` is a morphism of schemes (or more generally
21	a morphism of commutative ringed spaces), we would like to apply
22	these definitions in the case where `F` is the pullback functor from
23	open subsets of `S` to open subsets of `X` and `φ` is the
24	morphism $O_S ⟶ f_* O_X$.
25	
26	In order to prove that there exists a universal derivation, the target
27	of which shall be called the presheaf of relative differentials of `φ`,
28	we first study the case where `F` is the identity functor.
29	In this case where we have a morphism of presheaves of commutative
30	rings `φ' : S' ⟶ R`, we construct a derivation
31	`DifferentialsConstruction.derivation'` which is universal.
32	Then, the general case (TODO) shall be obtained by observing that
33	derivations for `S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R` identify to derivations
34	for `S' ⟶ R` where `S'` is the pullback by `F` of the presheaf of
35	commutative rings `S` (the data is the same: it suffices
36	to show that the two vanishing conditions `d_app` are equivalent).
37	
38	-/
39	
40	@[expose] public section
41	
42	universe v u v₁ v₂ u₁ u₂
43	
44	open CategoryTheory
45	
46	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} D]
47	
48	namespace PresheafOfModules
49	
50	variable {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}} {F : C ⥤ D} {S' R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
51	  (M N : PresheafOfModules.{v} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
52	  (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R) (φ' : S' ⟶ R)
53	
54	/-- Given a morphism of presheaves of commutative rings `φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R`,
55	this is the type of relative `φ`-derivation of a presheaf of `R`-modules `M`. -/
56	@[ext]
57	structure Derivation where
58	  /-- the underlying additive map `R.obj X →+ M.obj X` of a derivation -/
59	  d {X : Dᵒᵖ} : R.obj X →+ M.obj X
60	  d_mul {X : Dᵒᵖ} (a b : R.obj X) : d (a * b) = a • d b + b • d a := by cat_disch
61	  d_map {X Y : Dᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y) (x : R.obj X) :
62	    d (R.map f x) = M.map f (d x) := by cat_disch
63	  d_app {X : Cᵒᵖ} (a : S.obj X) : d (φ.app X a) = 0 := by cat_disch
64	
65	namespace Derivation
66	
67	-- Note: `d_app` cannot be a simp lemma because `dsimp` would
68	-- simplify the composition of functors `R ⋙ forget₂ _ _`
69	attribute [simp] d_mul d_map
70	
71	variable {M N φ}
72	
73	lemma congr_d {d d' : M.Derivation φ} (h : d = d') {X : Dᵒᵖ} (b : R.obj X) :

The natural transformation typechecks! Now I need to prove the naturality condition. Let me work on that.

For `d' : M.Derivation' φ'`, the `d_map` field says:
```lean
d_map {X Y : Dᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y) (x : R.obj X) :
  d (R.map f x) = M.map f (d x)
```

In terms of `AddMonoidHom`, this means:
```lean
d.d (R.map f x) = M.map f (d.d x)
```

But `M.map f` here is a morphism in `ModuleCat`. And `((relativeDifferentials f).val.presheaf.map g)` is a morphism in `AddCommGrpCat` (or `Ab`). 

Also, `R.map f` is `X.presheaf.map g` (since `R = X.presheaf`). And `(X.ringCatSheaf.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat).map g` is `(forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat).map (X.ringCatSheaf.presheaf.map g)`.

I need to relate these. Since `X.ringCatSheaf.presheaf = X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat`, we have:
`X.ringCatSheaf.presheaf.map g = (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).map g = (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).map (X.presheaf.map g)`.

And `(X.ringCatSheaf.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat).map g = (forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat).map (X.ringCatSheaf.presheaf.map g)`.

For `d'.d (X := V) ((X.ringCatSheaf.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat).map g x)`, I need this to equal `(relativeDifferentials f).val.presheaf.map g ((d'.d (X := U)) x)`.

Using `d_map`: `d'.d (X.presheaf.map g x) = (relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).map g (d'.d x)`.

But `(relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).map g` is a morphism in `ModuleCat`. And `(relativeDifferentials f).val.presheaf.map g` is the same map but viewed as a morphism in `AddCommGrpCat`.

Also, `(X.ringCatSheaf.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat).map g x` should be the same as `X.presheaf.map g x` at the level of the underlying additive group.

import Mathlib.RingTheory.Kaehler.Basic
import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme
import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Differentials.Presheaf
import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf
import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Presheaf
import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Sheaf

universe u
open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
variable {X Y S : Scheme.{u}}

noncomputable def relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (f : X ⟶ S) : X.PresheafOfModules :=
  let φ' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm
    f.c
  PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials' φ'

theorem relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf (f : X ⟶ S) :
    Presheaf.IsSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat)
      (relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).presheaf := by
  sorry

noncomputable def relativeDifferentials (f : X ⟶ S) : X.Modules :=
  ⟨relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f, relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf f⟩

noncomputable def universalDerivation (f : X ⟶ S) :
    X.ringCatSheaf.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat ⟶
      (relativeDifferentials f).val.presheaf := by
  let φ' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm
    f.c
  let d' := PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.derivation' φ'
  have h_eq : X.ringCatSheaf.presheaf = X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat := rfl
  refine {
    app := fun U ↦ AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (d'.d (X := U)),
    naturality := fun U V g ↦ ?naturality
  }
  case naturality =>
    ext x
    simp [AddCommGrpCat.hom_ofHom, h_eq]
    have h := d'.d_map g x
    -- dsimp should simplify the goal to match h
    dsimp at h ⊢
    exact h

end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
EOF

44	  SheafOfModules.forgetToSheafModuleCat (Spec R).ringCatSheaf (.op ⊤)
45	    (Limits.initialOpOfTerminal Limits.isTerminalTop) ⋙
46	  sheafCompose _ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (StructureSheaf.globalSectionsIso R).hom.hom)

48	/-- The global section functor for `𝒪_{Spec R}` modules -/
49	noncomputable
50	def moduleSpecΓFunctor : (Spec (.of R)).Modules ⥤ ModuleCat R :=
51	  modulesSpecToSheaf ⋙ TopCat.Sheaf.forget _ _ ⋙ (evaluation _ _).obj (.op ⊤)

53	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
54	open PrimeSpectrum in
55	/-- The forgetful functor from `𝒪_{Spec R}` modules to sheaves of `R`-modules is fully faithful. -/
56	def SpecModulesToSheafFullyFaithful : (modulesSpecToSheaf (R := R)).FullyFaithful where
57	  preimage {M N} f := ⟨fun U ↦ ModuleCat.ofHom ⟨(f.1.app U).hom.toAddHom, by
58	    intro t m
59	    apply TopCat.Presheaf.IsSheaf.section_ext (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj N).2
60	    intro x hxU
61	    obtain ⟨a, ⟨_, ⟨r, rfl⟩, rfl⟩, hxr, hrU : basicOpen _ ≤ _⟩ :=
62	      PrimeSpectrum.isBasis_basic_opens.exists_subset_of_mem_open hxU U.unop.2
63	    refine ⟨_, hrU, hxr, ?_⟩
64	    refine Eq.trans ?_ (N.val.map_smul (homOfLE hrU).op t _).symm
65	    change N.1.map (homOfLE hrU).op (f.1.app _ _) = _ • N.1.map (homOfLE hrU).op (f.1.app _ _)
66	    have (x : _) :
67	        f.1.app _ (M.1.map (homOfLE hrU).op _) = N.1.map (homOfLE hrU).op (f.1.app _ x) :=
68	      congr($(f.1.naturality (homOfLE hrU).op).hom x)
69	    rw [← this, ← this, M.val.map_smul]
70	    generalize (Spec R).ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE hrU).op t = t
71	    letI := Module.compHom (R := Γ(Spec R, basicOpen r)) Γ(M, basicOpen r)
72	      (algebraMap R Γ(Spec R, basicOpen r))
73	    haveI : IsScalarTower R Γ(Spec R, basicOpen r) Γ(M, basicOpen r) :=
74	      .of_algebraMap_smul fun _ _ ↦ rfl
75	    letI := Module.compHom Γ(N, basicOpen r) (algebraMap R Γ(Spec R, basicOpen r))
76	    haveI : IsScalarTower R Γ(Spec R, basicOpen r) Γ(N, basicOpen r) :=
77	      .of_algebraMap_smul fun _ _ ↦ rfl
78	    exact (IsLocalization.linearMap_compatibleSMul (.powers (M := R) r)
79	      Γ(Spec R, basicOpen r) Γ(M, basicOpen r) Γ(N, basicOpen r)).map_smul
80	      (f.hom.app _).hom _ _⟩, fun i ↦ by ext x; exact congr($(f.1.naturality i).hom x)⟩
81	  map_preimage f := rfl
82	  preimage_map f := rfl

84	/--
85	`M^~` as a sheaf of `𝒪_{Spec R}`-modules
86	-/
87	def tilde : (Spec R).Modules where
88	  val := moduleStructurePresheaf R M
89	  isSheaf := (TopCat.Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_comp (forget AddCommGrpCat) _).2
90	    (structureSheafInType R M).2

94	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
95	/-- (Implementation). The image of `tilde` under `modulesSpecToSheaf` is isomorphic to
96	`structurePresheafInModuleCat`. They are defeq as types but the `Smul` instance are not defeq. -/
97	noncomputable
98	def modulesSpecToSheafIso :
99	    (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).1 ≅ structurePresheafInModuleCat R M :=
100	  NatIso.ofComponents (fun U ↦ LinearEquiv.toModuleIso
101	    (X₁ := (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.obj _)
102	    { __ := AddEquiv.refl _,
103	      map_smul' r m := IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul (M := ((structureSheafInType R M).obj.obj U))
104	        ((structureSheafInType R R).obj.obj U) r m }) fun _ ↦ rfl
105	
106	/-- The map from `M` to `Γ(M, U)`. This is a localization map when `U = D(f)`. -/
107	def toOpen (U : (Spec R).Opens) : M ⟶ (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.obj (.op U) :=
108	  ModuleCat.ofHom (StructureSheaf.toOpenₗ R M U) ≫ ((modulesSpecToSheafIso M).app _).inv
109	
110	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
111	theorem toOpen_res (U V : Opens (PrimeSpectrum.Top R)) (i : V ⟶ U) :
112	    toOpen M U ≫ (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.map i.op = toOpen M V :=
113	  rfl
114	
115	instance (f : R) : IsLocalizedModule (.powers f) (toOpen M (basicOpen f)).hom :=
116	  .of_linearEquiv (.powers f) (StructureSheaf.toOpenₗ R M (basicOpen f))
117	    ((modulesSpecToSheafIso M).app _).toLinearEquiv.symm
118	
119	noncomputable
120	instance (x : PrimeSpectrum.Top R) : Module R ((tilde M).presheaf.stalk x) :=
121	  inferInstanceAs (Module R ↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk (moduleStructurePresheaf R M).presheaf x))
122	
123	/--
124	If `x` is a point of `Spec R`, this is the morphism of `R`-modules from `M` to the stalk of
125	`M^~` at `x`.
126	-/
127	noncomputable def toStalk (x : PrimeSpectrum.Top R) :
128	    ModuleCat.of R M ⟶ ModuleCat.of R ((tilde M).presheaf.stalk x) :=
129	  ModuleCat.ofHom (StructureSheaf.toStalkₗ ..)
130	
131	instance (x : PrimeSpectrum.Top R) :
132	    IsLocalizedModule x.asIdeal.primeCompl (toStalk M x).hom :=
133	  inferInstanceAs (IsLocalizedModule x.asIdeal.primeCompl (StructureSheaf.toStalkₗ ..))
134	
135	/-- The tilde construction is functorial. -/
136	protected noncomputable def map {M N : ModuleCat R} (f : M ⟶ N) : tilde M ⟶ tilde N :=
137	  SpecModulesToSheafFullyFaithful.preimage ⟨(modulesSpecToSheafIso M).hom ≫
138	    { app U := ModuleCat.ofHom (StructureSheaf.comapₗ f.hom _ _ .rfl) } ≫
139	    (modulesSpecToSheafIso N).inv⟩

141	@[simp, reassoc]
142	protected lemma map_id {M : ModuleCat R} : tilde.map (𝟙 M) = 𝟙 _ := by
143	  ext p x
144	  exact Subtype.ext (funext fun y ↦ DFunLike.congr_fun (LocalizedModule.map_id _) _)
145	
146	@[simp, reassoc]
147	protected lemma map_comp {M N P : ModuleCat R} (f : M ⟶ N) (g : N ⟶ P) :
148	    tilde.map (f ≫ g) = tilde.map f ≫ tilde.map g := by
149	  ext p x
150	  exact Subtype.ext (funext
151	    fun y ↦ DFunLike.congr_fun (IsLocalizedModule.map_comp' y.1.asIdeal.primeCompl
152	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap y.1.asIdeal.primeCompl M)
153	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap y.1.asIdeal.primeCompl N)
154	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap y.1.asIdeal.primeCompl P) _ _) _)
155	
156	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
157	lemma toOpen_map_app {M N : ModuleCat R} (f : M ⟶ N)
158	    (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens (PrimeSpectrum R)) :
159	    toOpen M U ≫ (modulesSpecToSheaf.map (tilde.map f)).1.app _ =
160	    f ≫ toOpen N U := by
161	  ext x; exact Subtype.ext (funext fun y ↦ IsLocalizedModule.map_apply y.1.asIdeal.primeCompl
162	    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap y.1.asIdeal.primeCompl M)
163	     (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap y.1.asIdeal.primeCompl N) _ x)

182	lemma isUnit_algebraMap_end_basicOpen (M : (Spec (.of R)).Modules) (f : R) :
183	    IsUnit (algebraMap R (Module.End R
184	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.obj (.op (basicOpen f)))) f) := by
185	  rw [Module.End.isUnit_iff]
186	  change Function.Bijective (algebraMap Γ(Spec R, basicOpen f)
187	      (Module.End Γ(Spec R, basicOpen f) Γ(M, basicOpen f)) (algebraMap R _ f))
188	  rw [← Module.End.isUnit_iff]
189	  exact (IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit _).map _

193	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
194	/-- This is the counit of the tilde-Gamma adjunction. -/
195	noncomputable def Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ (M : (Spec (.of R)).Modules) :
196	    tilde ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.obj (.op ⊤)) ⟶ M :=
197	  SpecModulesToSheafFullyFaithful.preimage
198	    ⟨TopCat.Sheaf.restrictHomEquivHom _ _ isBasis_basic_opens
199	    { app (f : Rᵒᵖ) := by
200	        refine (ModuleCat.ofHom (IsLocalizedModule.lift (.powers (M := R) f.unop)
201	          (tilde.toOpen _ (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f.unop)).hom
202	          ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op).hom ?_):)
203	        rw [Subtype.forall]
204	        change Submonoid.powers _ ≤ (IsUnit.submonoid _).comap _
205	        simp only [inducedFunctor_obj, Submonoid.powers_le, Submonoid.mem_comap]
206	        exact tilde.isUnit_algebraMap_end_basicOpen M f.unop
207	      naturality {f g : Rᵒᵖ} i := by
208	        letI N := (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.obj (.op ⊤)
209	        ext1
210	        apply IsLocalizedModule.ext (.powers (M := R) f.unop)
211	          (tilde.toOpen _ (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (R := R) f.unop)).hom
212	        · rw [Subtype.forall]
213	          change Submonoid.powers _ ≤ (IsUnit.submonoid _).comap _
214	          simp only [Submonoid.powers_le, Submonoid.mem_comap, IsUnit.mem_submonoid_iff]
215	          obtain ⟨n, a, e⟩ : ∃ n, f.unop ∣ g.unop ^ n := by
216	            simpa only [Ideal.mem_radical_iff, Ideal.mem_span_singleton] using
217	              (basicOpen_le_basicOpen_iff _ _).mp (i.1.hom.le)
218	          refine ((Commute.isUnit_mul_iff (b := algebraMap R _ a) (.map (.all _ _) _)).mp ?_).1
219	          rw [← map_mul, ← e, map_pow]
220	          exact (tilde.isUnit_algebraMap_end_basicOpen M g.unop).pow n
221	        · dsimp [← ModuleCat.hom_comp]
222	          rw [tilde.toOpen_res_assoc]
223	          ext x
224	          dsimp
225	          simp only [IsLocalizedModule.lift_apply, ← ModuleCat.comp_apply, ← Functor.map_comp]
226	          rfl }⟩

229	@[reassoc]
230	lemma Scheme.Modules.toOpen_fromTildeΓ_app (M : (Spec (.of R)).Modules) (U) :
231	    tilde.toOpen ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.obj (.op ⊤)) U ≫
232	      (modulesSpecToSheaf.map M.fromTildeΓ).1.app (.op U) =
233	    (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).1.map (homOfLE le_top).op := by
234	  wlog hU : U = PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen 1 generalizing U
235	  · rw [← tilde.toOpen_res _ (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen 1) _ (homOfLE (by simp)), Category.assoc,
236	      NatTrans.naturality, ← Category.assoc, this, ← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, homOfLE_comp]
237	    simp
238	  subst hU
239	  simp only [fromTildeΓ, inducedFunctor_obj,
240	    homOfLE_leOfHom, Functor.FullyFaithful.map_preimage, TopCat.Sheaf.extend_hom_app]
241	  ext x
242	  refine (IsLocalizedModule.lift_apply (.powers (M := R) 1)
243	    (tilde.toOpen _ (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (R := R) 1)).hom
244	    ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op).hom (by simp) x)
245	
246	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
247	/-- This is the counit of the tilde-Gamma adjunction. -/
248	noncomputable def Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓNatTrans :
249	    moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R) ⋙ tilde.functor (R := R) ⟶ 𝟭 _ where
250	  app := fromTildeΓ
251	  naturality {M N} f := by
252	    apply SpecModulesToSheafFullyFaithful.map_injective
253	    apply CategoryTheory.Sheaf.hom_ext
254	    apply (TopCat.Sheaf.restrictHomEquivHom _ _ PrimeSpectrum.isBasis_basic_opens).symm.injective
255	    ext r : 3
256	    apply IsLocalizedModule.ext (.powers (M := R) r.unop)
257	      (tilde.toOpen ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.obj (.op ⊤))
258	        (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (R := R) r.unop)).hom
259	    · rw [Subtype.forall]
260	      change Submonoid.powers _ ≤ (IsUnit.submonoid _).comap _
261	      simp only [Submonoid.powers_le, Submonoid.mem_comap, IsUnit.mem_submonoid_iff]
262	      exact tilde.isUnit_algebraMap_end_basicOpen ..
263	    dsimp [TopCat.Sheaf.restrictHomEquivHom, Functor.IsCoverDense.restrictHomEquivHom,
264	      moduleSpecΓFunctor, Sheaf.forget]
265	    simp only [← ModuleCat.hom_comp, Functor.map_comp]
266	    congr 1
267	    erw [tilde.toOpen_map_app_assoc, toOpen_fromTildeΓ_app N (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r.unop),
268	      toOpen_fromTildeΓ_app_assoc M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r.unop),
269	      ← (modulesSpecToSheaf.map f).hom.naturality]
270	
271	/-- `tilde.isoTop` bundled as a natural isomorphism.
272	This is the unit of the tilde-Gamma adjunction. -/
273	def tilde.toTildeΓNatIso : 𝟭 _ ≅ tilde.functor R ⋙ moduleSpecΓFunctor :=
274	  NatIso.ofComponents tilde.isoTop fun f ↦ (tilde.toOpen_map_app f _).symm
275	
276	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
277	open Scheme.Modules in
278	/-- The tilde-Gamma adjunction. -/
279	def tilde.adjunction : tilde.functor R ⊣ moduleSpecΓFunctor where
280	  unit := toTildeΓNatIso.hom
281	  counit := fromTildeΓNatTrans
282	  left_triangle_components M := by
283	    apply SpecModulesToSheafFullyFaithful.map_injective
284	    apply CategoryTheory.Sheaf.hom_ext
285	    apply (TopCat.Sheaf.restrictHomEquivHom _ _ PrimeSpectrum.isBasis_basic_opens).symm.injective
286	    ext r : 3
287	    apply IsLocalizedModule.ext (.powers (M := R) r.unop)
288	      (toOpen _ (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (R := R) r.unop)).hom
289	    · rw [Subtype.forall]
290	      change Submonoid.powers _ ≤ (IsUnit.submonoid _).comap _
291	      simp only [Submonoid.powers_le, Submonoid.mem_comap, IsUnit.mem_submonoid_iff]
292	      exact isUnit_algebraMap_end_basicOpen ..
293	    dsimp [toTildeΓNatIso, isoTop,
294	      TopCat.Sheaf.restrictHomEquivHom, Functor.IsCoverDense.restrictHomEquivHom,
295	      fromTildeΓNatTrans, moduleSpecΓFunctor, Sheaf.forget, sheafToPresheaf]
296	    simp only [← ModuleCat.hom_comp, Functor.map_comp]
297	    congr 1
298	    rw [ObjectProperty.FullSubcategory.comp_hom]
299	    dsimp
300	    rw [toOpen_map_app_assoc, toOpen_fromTildeΓ_app]
301	    rfl
302	  right_triangle_components M := by
303	    dsimp [toTildeΓNatIso, fromTildeΓNatTrans, tilde.isoTop, moduleSpecΓFunctor, Sheaf.forget]
304	    rw [toOpen_fromTildeΓ_app]
305	    exact (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).obj.map_id _
306	
307	instance : IsIso (tilde.adjunction (R := R)).unit := by
308	  dsimp [tilde.adjunction]; infer_instance
309	
310	/-- The tilde functor is fully faithful. We will later show that the essential image is
311	exactly quasi-coherent modules. -/
312	def tilde.fullyFaithfulFunctor : (tilde.functor R).FullyFaithful :=
313	  tilde.adjunction.fullyFaithfulLOfIsIsoUnit
314	
315	instance : (tilde.functor R).Full := tilde.fullyFaithfulFunctor.full
316	instance : (tilde.functor R).Faithful := tilde.fullyFaithfulFunctor.faithful
317	instance : (tilde.functor R).IsLeftAdjoint := tilde.adjunction.isLeftAdjoint
318	instance : (tilde.functor R).Additive :=
319	  have := Limits.preservesBinaryBiproducts_of_preservesBinaryCoproducts (tilde.functor R)
320	  Functor.additive_of_preservesBinaryBiproducts _

344	section IsQuasicoherent
345	
346	open Limits
347	
348	/-- Tilde of `R` as an `R`-module is isomorphic to the structure sheaf `𝒪_{Spec R}`. -/
349	noncomputable
350	def tildeSelf : tilde (ModuleCat.of R R) ≅ SheafOfModules.unit.{u} _ := .refl _
351	
352	instance : IsIso (Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ (SheafOfModules.unit.{u} (Spec R).ringCatSheaf)) :=
353	  isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff.mpr ⟨_, ⟨tildeSelf⟩⟩
354	
355	/-- Tilde of direct sums of `R` as an `R`-module is isomorphic to the free sheaf. -/
356	noncomputable
357	def tildeFinsupp (ι : Type u) : tilde (ModuleCat.of R (ι →₀ R)) ≅ SheafOfModules.free.{u} ι :=
358	  letI H : IsColimit <| (tilde.functor R).mapCocone (ModuleCat.finsuppCocone R R ι) :=
359	    isColimitOfPreserves (tilde.functor R) (ModuleCat.finsuppCoconeIsColimit R R ι)
360	  letI iso : (Discrete.functor fun (_ : ι) ↦ ModuleCat.of R R) ⋙ tilde.functor R ≅
361	         Discrete.functor fun _ ↦ SheafOfModules.unit.{u} _ :=
362	      Discrete.natIso (fun _ ↦ tildeSelf)
363	  IsColimit.coconePointUniqueUpToIso
364	    ((IsColimit.precomposeHomEquiv iso.symm _).symm H) (coproductIsCoproduct _)
365	
366	instance (ι : Type u) :
367	    IsIso (Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ (R := R) (SheafOfModules.free.{u} ι)) :=
368	  isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff.mpr ⟨_, ⟨tildeFinsupp _⟩⟩
369	
370	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
371	/-- Given a presentation of a module `M`, we may construct an associated presentation of `M^~`. -/
372	noncomputable
373	def presentationTilde (s : Set M) (hs : Submodule.span R s = ⊤)
374	    (t : Set (s →₀ R))
375	    (ht : Submodule.span R t = LinearMap.ker (Finsupp.linearCombination R ((↑) : s → M))) :
376	    (tilde M).Presentation := by
377	  haveI H₁ : Function.Exact
378	      (ModuleCat.ofHom (Finsupp.linearCombination (α := t) R (↑)))
379	      (ModuleCat.ofHom (Finsupp.linearCombination (α := s) (M := M) R (↑))) :=
380	    (LinearMap.exact_iff.mpr (by simp [Finsupp.range_linearCombination, ht]))
381	  refine SheafOfModules.presentationOfIsCokernelFree.{u}
382	      ((tildeFinsupp t).inv ≫ tilde.map (ModuleCat.ofHom (Finsupp.linearCombination R (↑))) ≫
383	        (tildeFinsupp s).hom) ((tildeFinsupp s).inv ≫
384	          tilde.map (ModuleCat.ofHom (Finsupp.linearCombination R (↑)))) (by
385	    simp only [Category.assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc, Preadditive.IsIso.comp_left_eq_zero]
386	    rw [← tilde.map_comp, ← ModuleCat.ofHom_comp]
387	    convert tilde.map_zero
388	    exact congr(ModuleCat.ofHom $(H₁.linearMap_comp_eq_zero))) ?_
389	  letI h₁ := ModuleCat.isColimitCokernelCofork _ _ H₁
390	    (by simp [← LinearMap.range_eq_top, Finsupp.range_linearCombination, hs])
391	  refine IsCokernel.ofIso _ (CokernelCofork.mapIsColimit _ h₁ (tilde.functor R)) _ (tildeFinsupp t)
392	    (tildeFinsupp s) (.refl _) (by simp) (by simp)
393	
394	instance : (tilde M).IsQuasicoherent :=
395	  (presentationTilde.{u} _ .univ (by simp) _ (Submodule.span_eq _)).isQuasicoherent
396	
397	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
398	lemma isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation (M : (Spec R).Modules) (P : M.Presentation) :
399	    IsIso M.fromTildeΓ := by
400	  rw [isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff]
401	  let g := (tilde.functor _).preimage <| (tildeFinsupp _).hom ≫ P.relations.π ≫ kernel.ι _ ≫
402	    (tildeFinsupp _).inv
403	  let iso : cokernel ((tilde.functor R).map g) ≅ cokernel (P.relations.π ≫ kernel.ι _) := by
404	    refine cokernel.mapIso _ _ (tildeFinsupp _) (tildeFinsupp _) ?_
405	    simp only [g, (tilde.functor R).map_preimage]
406	    simp
407	  exact ⟨cokernel g, ⟨PreservesCokernel.iso (tilde.functor R) g ≪≫ iso ≪≫
408	    IsColimit.coconePointUniqueUpToIso (colimit.isColimit _) P.isColimit⟩⟩
409	
410	end IsQuasicoherent

14	/-!
15	# Dense subsites
16	
17	We define `IsCoverDense` functors into sites as functors such that there exists a covering sieve
18	that factors through images of the functor for each object in `D`.
19	
20	## Main results
21	
22	- `CategoryTheory.Functor.IsCoverDense.Types.presheafHom`: If `G : C ⥤ (D, K)` is locally-full
23	  and cover-dense, then given any presheaf `ℱ` and sheaf `ℱ'` on `D`,
24	  and a morphism `α : G ⋙ ℱ ⟶ G ⋙ ℱ'`, we may glue them together to obtain
25	  a morphism of presheaves `ℱ ⟶ ℱ'`.
26	- `CategoryTheory.Functor.IsCoverDense.sheafIso`: If `ℱ` above is a sheaf and `α` is an iso,
27	  then the result is also an iso.
28	- `CategoryTheory.Functor.IsCoverDense.iso_of_restrict_iso`: If `G : C ⥤ (D, K)` is locally-full
29	  and cover-dense, then given any sheaves `ℱ, ℱ'` on `D`, and a morphism `α : ℱ ⟶ ℱ'`,
30	  then `α` is an iso if `G ⋙ ℱ ⟶ G ⋙ ℱ'` is iso.
31	- `CategoryTheory.Functor.IsDenseSubsite`:
32	  The functor `G : C ⥤ D` exhibits `(C, J)` as a dense subsite of `(D, K)` if `G` is cover-dense,
33	  locally fully-faithful, and `S` is a cover of `C` iff the image of `S` in `D` is a cover.
34	- `CategoryTheory.Functor.IsDenseSubsite.sheafEquiv`: the equivalence of
35	  categories `Sheaf J A ≌ Sheaf K A` when `(C, J)` is a dense subsite of `(D, K)` and
36	  the pushforward functor `Sheaf K A ⥤ Sheaf J A` is an equivalence, which we show
37	  in two situations:
38	  * the sites are small and `A` has suitable limits (see the file
39	    `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/DenseSubsite/SheafEquiv.lean`).
40	  * the category `A` has limits of size `w` and `G` is `1`-hypercover cover dense
41	    relatively to the universe `w` (see the file
42	    `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/DenseSubsite/OneHypercoverDense.lean`).
43	
44	## References
45	
46	* [Elephant]: *Sketches of an Elephant*, ℱ. T. Johnstone: C2.2.
47	* https://ncatlab.org/nlab/show/dense+sub-site
48	* https://ncatlab.org/nlab/show/comparison+lemma
49	
50	-/

15	
16	In this file, we connect coverings in a topological space to sieves in the associated Grothendieck
17	topology, in preparation of connecting the sheaf condition on sites to the various sheaf conditions
18	on spaces.
19	
20	We also specialize results about sheaves on sites to sheaves on spaces; we show that the inclusion
21	functor from a topological basis to `TopologicalSpace.Opens` is cover dense, that open maps

39	/-- Given a presieve `R` on `U`, we obtain a covering family of open sets in `X`, by taking as index
40	type the type of dependent pairs `(V, f)`, where `f : V ⟶ U` is in `R`.
41	-/
42	def coveringOfPresieve (U : Opens X) (R : Presieve U) : (Σ V, { f : V ⟶ U // R f }) → Opens X :=
43	  fun f => f.1
44	
45	@[simp]
46	theorem coveringOfPresieve_apply (U : Opens X) (R : Presieve U) (f : Σ V, { f : V ⟶ U // R f }) :
47	    coveringOfPresieve U R f = f.1 := rfl

51	variable (U : Opens X) (R : Presieve U)
52	
53	/-- If `R` is a presieve in the Grothendieck topology on `Opens X`, the covering family associated
54	to `R` really is _covering_, i.e. the union of all open sets equals `U`.
55	-/
56	theorem iSup_eq_of_mem_grothendieck (hR : Sieve.generate R ∈ Opens.grothendieckTopology X U) :
57	    iSup (coveringOfPresieve U R) = U := by
58	  apply le_antisymm
59	  · refine iSup_le ?_
60	    intro f
61	    exact f.2.1.le
62	  intro x hxU
63	  rw [Opens.mem_iSup]
64	  obtain ⟨V, iVU, ⟨W, iVW, iWU, hiWU, -⟩, hxV⟩ := hR x hxU
65	  exact ⟨⟨W, ⟨iWU, hiWU⟩⟩, iVW.le hxV⟩
66	
67	end coveringOfPresieve

69	/-- Given a family of opens `U : ι → Opens X` and any open `Y : Opens X`, we obtain a presieve
70	on `Y` by declaring that a morphism `f : V ⟶ Y` is a member of the presieve if and only if
71	there exists an index `i : ι` such that `V = U i`.
72	-/
73	def presieveOfCoveringAux {ι : Type v} (U : ι → Opens X) (Y : Opens X) : Presieve Y :=
74	  fun V _ => ∃ i, V = U i
75	
76	/-- Take `Y` to be `iSup U` and obtain a presieve over `iSup U`. -/
77	def presieveOfCovering {ι : Type v} (U : ι → Opens X) : Presieve (iSup U) :=
78	  presieveOfCoveringAux U (iSup U)

101

105	def homOfIndex (i : ι) : Σ V, { f : V ⟶ iSup U // presieveOfCovering U f } :=

107

110	-/
111	def indexOfHom (f : Σ V, { f : V ⟶ iSup U // presieveOfCovering U f }) : ι :=
112	  f.2.2.choose
113	
114	theorem indexOfHom_spec (f : Σ V, { f : V ⟶ iSup U // presieveOfCovering U f }) :
115	    f.1 = U (indexOfHom U f) :=
116	  f.2.2.choose_spec

246	lemma pseudofunctor_associativity :
247	    (pullbackComp f (g ≫ h)).inv ≫
248	      Functor.whiskerRight (pullbackComp g h).inv _ ≫ (Functor.associator _ _ _).hom ≫
249	        Functor.whiskerLeft _ (pullbackComp f g).hom ≫ (pullbackComp (f ≫ g) h).hom =
250	    eqToHom (by simp) := by
251	  let e₁ := pullbackComp f (g ≫ h)
252	  let e₂ := Functor.isoWhiskerRight (pullbackComp g h) (pullback f)
253	  let e₃ := Functor.isoWhiskerLeft (pullback h) (pullbackComp f g)
254	  let e₄ := pullbackComp (f ≫ g) h
255	  change e₁.inv ≫ e₂.inv ≫ (Functor.associator _ _ _).hom ≫ e₃.hom ≫ e₄.hom = _
256	  have : e₃.hom ≫ e₄.hom = (Functor.associator _ _ _).inv ≫ e₂.hom ≫ e₁.hom :=
257	    congr_arg Iso.hom (SheafOfModules.pullback_assoc.{u}
258	      h.toRingCatSheafHom g.toRingCatSheafHom f.toRingCatSheafHom)

301	    Pseudofunctor (LocallyDiscrete Scheme.{u}ᵒᵖ) (Adj Cat) :=

316	/-- Restriction of an `𝒪ₓ`-module along an open immersion.
317	This is isomorphic to the pullback functor (see `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`)
318	but has better defeqs. -/
319	def restrictFunctor : Y.Modules ⥤ X.Modules :=
320	  letI α : X.presheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ Y.presheaf := { app U := (f.appIso U.unop).inv }
321	  SheafOfModules.pushforward (F := f.opensFunctor)
322	    ⟨Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)⟩
323	
324	/-- The restriction of a module along an open immersion. -/
325	abbrev restrict (M : Y.Modules) (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] : X.Modules :=
326	  (restrictFunctor f).obj M
327	
328	@[simp] lemma restrict_obj (M : Y.Modules) (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] (U) :
329	    Γ(M.restrict f, U) = Γ(M, f ''ᵁ U) := rfl
330	
331	@[simp] lemma restrict_map (M : Y.Modules) (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] {U V} (i : U ⟶ V) :
332	    (M.restrict f).presheaf.map i.op = M.presheaf.map (f.opensFunctor.map i).op := rfl
333	
334	/-- The restriction of a module along an open immersion. -/
335	def restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso : pushforward f ⋙ restrictFunctor f ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
336	  letI := CategoryTheory.Functor.isContinuous_comp.{u} f.opensFunctor (Opens.map f.base)
337	    (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (Opens.grothendieckTopology Y) (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
338	  (SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _) ≪≫ pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents
339	      (fun U ↦ eqToIso (f.preimage_image_eq U).symm) fun _ ↦ rfl) ≪≫
340	    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by ext U x; exact
341	      congr($(f.appIso_inv_app_presheafMap U.unop) x)) ≪≫ SheafOfModules.pushforwardId _
342	
343	/-- Restriction is right adjoint to pushforward. -/
344	def restrictAdjunction : restrictFunctor f ⊣ pushforward f := by
345	  refine pushforwardPushforwardAdj (by exact f.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction) _ _ ?_ ?_
346	  · ext U x; exact congr($((f.app_appIso_inv _).symm).hom x)
347	  · ext U x
348	    have : (f.appIso U.unop).inv ≫ f.app _ ≫
349	      X.presheaf.map (eqToHom (f.preimage_image_eq U.unop).symm).op = 𝟙 _ := by
350	      rw [Scheme.Hom.appIso_inv_app_assoc, ← Functor.map_comp, ← X.presheaf.map_id]; rfl
351	    exact congr($this x)
352	
353	instance : IsIso (restrictAdjunction f).counit :=
354	  inferInstanceAs (IsIso <| (restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso f).hom)
355	
356	instance : (restrictFunctor f).IsLeftAdjoint := (restrictAdjunction f).isLeftAdjoint
357	instance : (pushforward f).Full := (restrictAdjunction f).fullyFaithfulROfIsIsoCounit.full
358	instance : (pushforward f).Faithful := (restrictAdjunction f).fullyFaithfulROfIsIsoCounit.faithful

noncomputable def relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (f : X ⟶ S) : X.PresheafOfModules :=
  let φ' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm
    f.c
  PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials' φ'

theorem relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf (f : X ⟶ S) :
    Presheaf.IsSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat)
      (relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).presheaf := by
  sorry

noncomputable def relativeDifferentials (f : X ⟶ S) : X.Modules :=
  ⟨relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f, relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf f⟩

/-- Convert the sheaf of relative Kähler differentials of a curve over Spec k
    to a sheaf of k-modules. -/
noncomputable def relativeDifferentialsToModuleKPresheaf
    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
    (Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ ⥤ ModuleCat.{u} k where
  obj U := ModuleCat.of k (↑((relativeDifferentials C.hom).val.obj U))
  map {U V} f := sorry
  map_id U := sorry
  map_comp {U V W} f g := sorry

end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

42	def Hom (M N : X.Modules) : Type u := SheafOfModules.Hom M N
43	
44	instance : Category X.Modules where
45	  Hom := Modules.Hom
46	  __ := (inferInstance : Category (SheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf))
47	
48	noncomputable instance : Abelian X.Modules :=
49	  inferInstanceAs <| Abelian (SheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf)
50	instance : HasLimits X.Modules := inferInstanceAs (HasLimits (SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf))
51	instance : HasColimits X.Modules := inferInstanceAs (HasColimits (SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf))
52

57	This is mostly useful to transport results from (pre)sheaves of modules to `𝒪ₓ`-modules and
58	usually shouldn't be used directly when working with actual `𝒪ₓ`-modules. -/
59	def toPresheafOfModules : X.Modules ⥤ X.PresheafOfModules := SheafOfModules.forget _
60

62	def fullyFaithfulToPresheafOfModules : (Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).FullyFaithful :=
63	  SheafOfModules.fullyFaithfulForget _
64	
65	instance : (toPresheafOfModules X).Full := fullyFaithfulToPresheafOfModules.full
66	instance : (toPresheafOfModules X).Faithful := fullyFaithfulToPresheafOfModules.faithful
67	instance : (toPresheafOfModules X).IsRightAdjoint :=

69

72	noncomputable def toPresheaf : X.Modules ⥤ TopCat.Presheaf Ab X :=
73	  toPresheafOfModules X ⋙ PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _

14	# The category of `R`-modules
15	
16	`ModuleCat.{v} R` is the category of bundled `R`-modules with carrier in the universe `v`. We show
17	that it is preadditive and show that being an isomorphism, monomorphism and epimorphism is
18	equivalent to being a linear equivalence, an injective linear map and a surjective linear map,
19	respectively.
20	
21	## Implementation details
22	
23	To construct an object in the category of `R`-modules from a type `M` with an instance of the
24	`Module` typeclass, write `of R M`. There is a coercion in the other direction.
25	The roundtrip `↑(of R M)` is definitionally equal to `M` itself (when `M` is a type with `Module`
26	instance), and so is `of R ↑M` (when `M : ModuleCat R M`).
27	
28	The morphisms are given their own type, not identified with `LinearMap`.
29	There is a cast from morphisms in `Module R` to linear maps, written `f.hom` (`ModuleCat.Hom.hom`).
30	To go from linear maps to morphisms in `Module R`, use `ModuleCat.ofHom`.
31	
32	Similarly, given an isomorphism `f : M ≅ N` use `f.toLinearEquiv` and given a linear equiv
33	`f : M ≃ₗ[R] N`, use `f.toModuleIso`.
34	-/

```lean
/-! ## Restriction of scalars to k-modules -/

/-- The presheaf of `k`-modules obtained from an `O_C`-module by restriction of
scalars along the structure morphism `k → Γ(C, U)`. -/
noncomputable def moduleKPresheafOfModules
    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
    (M : C.left.Modules) :
    (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ ⥤ ModuleCat.{u} k where
  obj U := (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (kToSection C U).hom).obj (M.val.obj U)
  map {U V} f := ModuleCat.ofHom
    { toFun := fun x => M.val.map f x
      map_add' := fun x y => by simp
      map_smul' := fun r x => by
        simp only [ModuleCat.restrictScalars.smul_def, algebraMap_eq_kToSection]
        rw [M.val.map_smul f (algebraMap k _ r) x]
        rw [algebraMap_naturality (C := C) f r]
        rfl }
  map_id U := by
    ext x
    simp only [ModuleCat.hom_id, LinearMap.id_coe, id_eq]
    rw [M.val.map_id U]
    simp
  map_comp {U V W} f g := by
    ext x
    simp only [ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply]
    rw [M.val.map_comp f g]
    simp

/-- The presheaf of `k`-modules obtained by restriction of scalars is a sheaf:
its underlying presheaf of abelian groups coincides with that of the original
`O_C`-module. -/
lemma moduleKPresheafOfModules_isSheaf
    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
    (M : C.left.Modules) :
    Presheaf.IsSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
      (moduleKPresheafOfModules C M) := by
  rw [Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_forget _ _ (CategoryTheory.forget (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
  have h : (moduleKPresheafOfModules C M) ⋙ CategoryTheory.forget (ModuleCat.{u} k) =
    M.val.presheaf ⋙ CategoryTheory.forget AddCommGrpCat := rfl
  rw [h]
  rw [← Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_forget _ _ (CategoryTheory.forget AddCommGrpCat)]
  exact M.property

/-- The sheaf of `k`-modules obtained from an `O_C`-module by restriction of
scalars along the structure morphism. -/
noncomputable def moduleKSheafOfModules
    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
    (M : C.left.Modules) :
    Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k) :=
  ⟨moduleKPresheafOfModules C M, moduleKPresheafOfModules_isSheaf C M⟩
```

/-! ## Restriction of scalars to k-modules -/

/-- The presheaf of `k`-modules obtained from an `O_C`-module by restriction of
scalars along the structure morphism `k → Γ(C, U)`.

On each open `U`, the sections are the `O_C(U)`-module `M(U)` viewed as a
`k`-module via the algebra map `k → O_C(U)`. The restriction maps are `k`-linear
by the naturality of this algebra map. -/
noncomputable def moduleKPresheafOfModules
    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
    (M : C.left.Modules) :
    (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ ⥤ ModuleCat.{u} k where
  obj U := (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C U).hom).obj (M.val.obj U)
  map {U V} f := ModuleCat.ofHom
    { toFun := fun x => M.val.map f x
      map_add' := fun x y => by simp
      map_smul' := fun r x => by
        simp only [ModuleCat.restrictScalars.smul_def, toModuleKSheaf.algebraMap_eq_kToSection]
        rw [M.val.map_smul f (algebraMap k _ r) x]
        rw [toModuleKSheaf.algebraMap_naturality (C := C) f r]
        rfl }
  map_id U := by
    ext x
    simp only [ModuleCat.hom_id, LinearMap.id_coe, id_eq]
    rw [M.val.map_id U]
    simp
  map_comp {U V W} f g := by
    ext x
    simp only [ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply]
    rw [M.val.map_comp f g]
    simp

/-- The presheaf of `k`-modules obtained by restriction of scalars is a sheaf:
its underlying presheaf of abelian groups coincides with that of the original
`O_C`-module. -/
lemma moduleKPresheafOfModules_isSheaf
    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
    (M : C.left.Modules) :
    Presheaf.IsSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
      (moduleKPresheafOfModules C M) := by
  rw [Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_forget _ _ (CategoryTheory.forget (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
  have h : (moduleKPresheafOfModules C M) ⋙ CategoryTheory.forget (ModuleCat.{u} k) =
    M.val.presheaf ⋙ CategoryTheory.forget AddCommGrpCat := rfl
  rw [h]
  rw [← Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_forget _ _ (CategoryTheory.forget AddCommGrpCat)]
  exact M.property

/-- The sheaf of `k`-modules obtained from an `O_C`-module by restriction of
scalars along the structure morphism. -/
noncomputable def moduleKSheafOfModules
    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
    (M : C.left.Modules) :
    Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k) :=
  ⟨moduleKPresheafOfModules C M, moduleKPresheafOfModules_isSheaf C M⟩

/-! ## Serre duality genus equality -/

/-- For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C` over a field `k`,
the dimension of `H^0(C, Ω_{C/k})` equals the dimension of `H^1(C, 𝒪_C)`,
and both equal the genus `g(C)`.

This is the dimension-one case of Serre duality applied to the canonical
sheaf `ω_C = Ω_{C/k}`. -/
theorem serre_duality_genus {k : Type u} [Field k]
    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) [IsIntegral C.left] [IsProper C.hom]
    (hsmooth : Smooth C.hom) :
    Module.rank k (HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 0) =
      Module.rank k
        (HModule k (moduleKSheafOfModules C (relativeDifferentials C.hom)) 0) := by
  sorry

end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

20	If C is a category with a Grothendieck topology, we define the notion of a sheaf taking values in

22	noting that the presheaf of sets "defined above" can be seen in the comments between tags 00VQ and
23	00VR on the page <https://stacks.math.columbia.edu/tag/00VL>. The advantage of this definition is
24	that we need no assumptions whatsoever on `A` other than the assumption that the morphisms in `C`
25	and `A` live in the same universe.
26	
27	* An `A`-valued presheaf `P : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A` is defined to be a sheaf (for the topology `J`) iff for
28	  every `E : A`, the type-valued presheaves of sets given by sending `U : Cᵒᵖ` to `Hom_{A}(E, P U)`
29	  are all sheaves of sets, see `CategoryTheory.Presheaf.IsSheaf`.
30	* When `A = Type`, this recovers the basic definition of sheaves of sets, see
31	  `CategoryTheory.isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_of_type`.
32	* An alternate definition in terms of limits, unconditionally equivalent to the original one:
33	  see `CategoryTheory.Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isLimit`.
34	* An alternate definition when `C` is small, has pullbacks and `A` has products is given by an
35	  equalizer condition `CategoryTheory.Presheaf.IsSheaf'`. This is equivalent to the earlier

12	# Presheaves of modules over a presheaf of rings.
13	
14	Given a presheaf of rings `R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat`, we define the category `PresheafOfModules R`.
15	An object `M : PresheafOfModules R` consists of a family of modules
16	`M.obj X : ModuleCat (R.obj X)` for all `X : Cᵒᵖ`, together with the data, for all `f : X ⟶ Y`,
17	of a functorial linear map `M.map f` from `M.obj X` to the restriction
18	of scalars of `M.obj Y` via `R.map f`.
19	
20	
21	## Future work
22	
23	* Compare this to the definition as a presheaf of pairs `(R, M)` with specified first part.
24	* Compare this to the definition as a module object of the presheaf of rings
25	  thought of as a monoid object.
26	* Presheaves of modules over a presheaf of commutative rings form a monoidal category.
27	* Pushforward and pullback.
28	-/
29	
30	@[expose] public section
31	
32	universe v v₁ u₁ u
33	
34	open CategoryTheory LinearMap Opposite
35	
36	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}}

40	objects `obj X : ModuleCat (R.obj X)` for all `X : Cᵒᵖ` together with
41	functorial maps `obj X ⟶ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (R.map f)).obj (obj Y)`
42	for all `f : X ⟶ Y` in `Cᵒᵖ`. -/
43	structure PresheafOfModules where
44	  /-- a family of modules over `R.obj X` for all `X` -/
45	  obj (X : Cᵒᵖ) : ModuleCat.{v} (R.obj X)
46	  /-- the restriction maps of a presheaf of modules -/
